name: Boundary Refactor Wave A — Memory Authority
outcome_goal: The memory/ layer becomes a thin adapter over storage/postgres/ repositories; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE and no CREATE TABLE statements remain in memory/crud.py or memory/retrieval_telemetry.py; the retrieval_metrics table is created by a migration, not at runtime.
anti_requirements: Do NOT change the public API of memory/crud.py — existing callers keep working unchanged
Do NOT change the public API of memory/retrieval_telemetry.py (RetrievalMetric, TelemetrySummary, TelemetryStore) — existing callers keep working unchanged
Do NOT alter the table schemas (memory_entities, memory_edges, retrieval_metrics). This is a move, not a redesign.
Do NOT widen writes outside storage/postgres/ and Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/
Do NOT remove the auto-prune behaviour from TelemetryStore.record() — it must still trigger through the repository.

jobs.label: memory_authority_plan
jobs.description: Decide whether each memory table gets its own repository or whether to extend PostgresMemoryGraphRepository
jobs.outcome_goal: A written plan names every new/edited repository class, every method's signature, and the migration file number that will own retrieval_metrics DDL
jobs.prompt:
Read the memory authority surfaces and write a concrete plan before touching any code.

Focus files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/crud.py (current writer for memory_entities + memory_edges)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/retrieval_telemetry.py (current writer for retrieval_metrics, including DDL)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/repository.py (shared types like MemoryEdgeRef)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/types.py (Entity, Edge, EntityType, RelationType)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py (existing repo for maintenance ops)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/validators.py (validator helpers + exception classes)
- Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/__init__.py (public exports)
- Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/015_memory_graph.sql (canonical DDL for memory_entities + memory_edges)

Decide:
1. Do memory_entities + memory_edges CRUD ops go into PostgresMemoryGraphRepository (extend it) or a new PostgresMemoryCrudRepository? Pick whichever keeps the public surface smallest.
2. Does retrieval_metrics get its own PostgresMemoryTelemetryRepository in a new file storage/postgres/memory_telemetry_repository.py? (Likely yes — different table family.)
3. What is the next available migration number in Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/? Name the new migration file explicitly.
4. List every function in memory/crud.py and memory/retrieval_telemetry.py that currently runs SQL, and map each to a repository method signature.

Return the plan as a structured summary that the next jobs can consume. Do not write code.

jobs.label: add_retrieval_metrics_migration
jobs.description: Move the retrieval_metrics CREATE TABLE DDL out of Python into a new migration
jobs.outcome_goal: retrieval_metrics is created by the migration runner on bootstrap; no Python runtime code issues CREATE TABLE for this table
jobs.prompt:
Add a new migration under Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/ that owns the retrieval_metrics DDL currently embedded in memory/retrieval_telemetry.py.

Steps:
1. Identify the next available migration number (look at existing files in Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/ — highest numeric prefix + 1; if >100 range is in use, pick the next in that range consistent with recent files).
2. Create the migration file with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS retrieval_metrics matching exactly the DDL at memory/retrieval_telemetry.py:40-52 (columns: id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, query_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL, pattern_name TEXT NOT NULL, result_count INTEGER NOT NULL, score_min/score_max/score_mean/score_stddev DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL, tie_break_count INTEGER NOT NULL, latency_ms DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL).
3. Add any indexes the query paths imply: pattern_name lookups, timestamp range scans. Check the SELECT paths in retrieval_telemetry.py:113-151 — add indexes that support them.
4. Do NOT edit memory/retrieval_telemetry.py yet — that happens in the next job. Only add the migration.

Verify the migration parses by running the migration runner in dry-run against a dev DB, or at minimum open a psql connection and run the migration SQL manually to confirm no syntax errors.
jobs.verify_command: psql postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis -c '\d retrieval_metrics' && cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available; ensure_postgres_available()'

jobs.label: create_memory_telemetry_repository
jobs.description: Move retrieval_metrics INSERT/SELECT/DELETE out of memory/retrieval_telemetry.py into a new repository
jobs.outcome_goal: TelemetryStore becomes a thin adapter that delegates all SQL to PostgresMemoryTelemetryRepository; zero SQL strings remain in memory/retrieval_telemetry.py
jobs.prompt:
Create Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/memory_telemetry_repository.py that owns every SQL statement currently in memory/retrieval_telemetry.py.

Steps:
1. Mirror the idiom from Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py — class name PostgresMemoryTelemetryRepository, __init__(conn), explicit method names.
2. Move these queries verbatim (but refactored into repository methods):
   - INSERT INTO retrieval_metrics (record_metric at line 71-86)
   - SELECT * FROM retrieval_metrics WHERE ... (query_metrics at line 113-151)
   - Aggregate SELECT for summary (line 134-151)
   - DISTINCT pattern_name query (line 148-151)
   - COUNT(*) query (line 163-171)
   - DELETE FROM retrieval_metrics (prune at line 172-176)
3. Use _require_text / PostgresWriteError from storage.postgres.validators for input validation.
4. Export PostgresMemoryTelemetryRepository via Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/__init__.py.
5. Rewrite memory/retrieval_telemetry.py TelemetryStore so __init__ no longer runs DDL and every method delegates to the repository. Remove the _DDL constant entirely.
6. Preserve every public identifier: RetrievalMetric, TelemetrySummary, TelemetryStore with the same method signatures.
7. Preserve auto-prune behaviour — TelemetryStore.record() still calls prune() after insert, but both go through the repository.

Verify:
- grep 'INSERT\|UPDATE\|DELETE\|CREATE TABLE' memory/retrieval_telemetry.py returns zero matches.
- py_compile succeeds on both files.
- The existing telemetry tests pass.
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -m py_compile 'Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/retrieval_telemetry.py' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/memory_telemetry_repository.py' && ! grep -E 'INSERT |UPDATE |DELETE FROM|CREATE TABLE' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/memory/retrieval_telemetry.py'

jobs.label: move_memory_crud_writes
jobs.description: Move memory/crud.py INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE statements into storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py (or a new crud repository)
jobs.outcome_goal: memory/crud.py contains only data marshalling and repository delegation; all SQL lives in storage/postgres/
jobs.prompt:
Move the SQL in memory/crud.py into storage/postgres/. Follow the plan from memory_authority_plan.

Current SQL writers in memory/crud.py:
- insert_entity (line 57-70) — INSERT INTO memory_entities with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE
- update_entity (line 85-117) — dynamic UPDATE memory_entities
- delete_entity (line 120-128) — UPDATE memory_entities SET archived=true
- add_edge (line 170-183) — INSERT INTO memory_edges with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE
- remove_edge (line 186-196) — DELETE FROM memory_edges

Steps:
1. If the plan chose to extend PostgresMemoryGraphRepository, add methods: upsert_entity, update_entity_fields, archive_entity, upsert_edge, delete_edge.
2. If the plan chose a new PostgresMemoryCrudRepository, create Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/memory_crud_repository.py following the idiom from memory_graph_repository.py.
3. Keep the SELECT helpers (get_entity, list_entities, search_entities, get_edges) where they are for now — this wave is about writes. The read-side will be picked up in a later sweep if needed.
4. Rewrite memory/crud.py so insert_entity, update_entity, delete_entity, add_edge, remove_edge delegate to the repository. Keep the same public signatures and return types — callers must not change.
5. Use the existing _row_to_entity / _row_to_edge helpers where the repository returns rows.
6. Update Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/__init__.py to export the new class/methods if a new class was created.

Verify:
- grep 'INSERT \|UPDATE \|DELETE FROM' memory/crud.py returns zero matches (reads only).
- py_compile succeeds on all changed files.
- The existing memory CRUD tests pass.

jobs.label: memory_authority_tests
jobs.description: Prove the memory authority migration with tests
jobs.outcome_goal: Targeted tests cover the new repository methods and the thin adapters; the existing memory test suite passes unchanged
jobs.prompt:
Add or extend tests proving the memory authority migration did not regress.

Steps:
1. Find existing tests under Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/ that exercise memory/crud.py and memory/retrieval_telemetry.py. Run them.
2. Add at least one test per new repository that round-trips a write and a read (insert + query).
3. Add a regression test that fails if any SQL string reappears in memory/crud.py or memory/retrieval_telemetry.py. Keep it simple: open the file, read as text, assert 'INSERT ' not in text etc.
4. Run the full memory test suite to confirm no semantic drift.

Do NOT invent new tables or columns. The regression tests are negative assertions — they only need to catch future backsliding.

jobs.label: memory_authority_close_bugs
jobs.description: Resolve BUG-B981792C01F4 and BUG-EB861DA883EF as FIXED once tests are green
jobs.outcome_goal: Both boundary bugs are marked FIXED with a resolution that cites this queue_id
jobs.prompt:
Mark the two linked bugs as FIXED after verifying the grep checks still pass.

Run both of:

```bash
export WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL="postgresql://localhost:5432/praxis"
cd /Users/nate/Praxis
"Code&DBs/Workflow/workflow" tools call praxis_bugs --input-json '{
  "command": "resolve",
  "bug_id": "BUG-B981792C01F4",
  "status": "FIXED",
  "resolution": "Moved retrieval_metrics DDL to a migration and INSERT/DELETE/SELECT into storage/postgres/memory_telemetry_repository.py in boundary_refactor_wave_a_memory_20260413. Verified no SQL remains in memory/retrieval_telemetry.py."
}' --yes

"Code&DBs/Workflow/workflow" tools call praxis_bugs --input-json '{
  "command": "resolve",
  "bug_id": "BUG-EB861DA883EF",
  "status": "FIXED",
  "resolution": "Moved memory_entities + memory_edges writes into storage/postgres/ in boundary_refactor_wave_a_memory_20260413. Verified no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE remains in memory/crud.py."
}' --yes
```

Only run this if both grep-based regression tests from memory_authority_tests pass.

jobs.label: memory_authority_review
jobs.description: Review the memory authority rewrite for semantic drift, missed callsites, and stale DDL
jobs.outcome_goal: Any remaining leaks or regressions are called out in writing before the wave is declared done
jobs.prompt:
Review the memory authority rewrite as a code review.

Prioritize findings on:
- any residual SQL strings in memory/crud.py or memory/retrieval_telemetry.py
- callsites of the moved functions that still expect the old behaviour (e.g. sync vs. async, exception types, return shapes)
- lost auto-prune behaviour or silent swallowed exceptions in add_edge
- any memory table DDL still being created at runtime anywhere in memory/ or runtime/
- test coverage gaps for the new repository methods
- whether memory/crud.py public API is unchanged for existing callers

Findings first. If no findings remain, say so explicitly.
